brand.alt: Photo of Damien Simone working the Estate's plot

content.p1: Le Calvaire is one of the steepest hillsides in the Canton of Geneva, facing due west, impossible to mechanize. A 40% slope, scorching in summer, slippery in winter, every gesture is done by hand, with effort, without shortcuts. It is this character that earned it its name. And it is this same character that, for a long time, drove many of our peers to prefer the neighboring plots.
content.p2: Damien Simone arrived even before being the owner. On this bare, uprooted, raw plot, he started by plowing it by hand, alone, even before the signature was placed. Then he replanted 900 carefully chosen vines: Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, grape varieties selected for their resilience against the climatic challenges of tomorrow.
content.p3: A work of meticulous observation, listening, and understanding of the living world. Additions of organic amendments to awaken a soil compacted for too long. And patience, that of letting things happen, of not forcing, of understanding that the earth cannot be corrected in a single season. A work that is seemingly manual but deeply intellectual, made possible by his training as an engineer in viticulture and oenology, which taught him to read the living world as much as to respect it. The vine responded. The soil became grassed. Lizards, pollinators, and cover crops returned.
content.p4: What the Calvaire takes, it gives back to us. The first harvests spoke for themselves. A quality that cannot be invented, that can be read in the bunch even before being tasted in the glass. And the winemakers of Russin, who had watched us arrive with the curiosity reserved for strangers, cast a different look on this hillside they had known forever.
content.p5: This is why we chose to give this name to our entire range. Not out of nostalgia, but because everything we do starts there, on this slope, in this effort, and that will not change.

images.p2: Image of a recently planted vine
images.p3: Image of a flower on the plot
images.p4: Beautiful bunch of grapes held by Damien Simone

brand.title: The legacy of a forgotten plot.

text.intro:
Our plots are classified as AOC Premier Cru Côtes de Russin.



This terroir, one of the most demanding in Geneva, is built around three distinct characteristics: stony clay-silt soils, pronounced slopes shaped by the Allondon river, and a due West exposure that defines the character of each vintage.
text.outro: Half of our vines are old vines. Their roots have long since bypassed the surface to seek out what the depth of the soil has to offer. What they bring up is a complexity that no cellar technique can manufacture. It has been there, in the earth, since before Damien Simone decided to make something of it.

slope.text: Then, the slopes. 30 to 40%. Steep. The vine works, roots deeply, searches. This controlled stress is exactly what concentrates the substance and provides length on the palate. No easy yields. No dilution.

soil.text: First, the soils. A mixture of clay, silt, and stones—a direct legacy of glaciations and Tertiary molasse. The clay retains just the right amount of water. The stones drain the rest. The vine never lacks anything, but it never receives too much. It is this tension that builds the precision and minerality found in the glass.

sun.alt: Photo of a bunch of grapes from the vineyard
sun.text: Finally, the exposure. Due West. The late afternoon sun, long and oblique, which ripens slowly without ever burning. A complete ripeness, built with patience, which explains the balance between freshness and volume that our wines consistently develop.

domain.title:
Geneva is not a detail.

It is the subject.

calvaire.alt: Photo taken at ground level of the plot
calvaire.text:
Everything starts with a slope. A 40% incline, facing due west, on one of the steepest hillsides in Russin.



Where others gave up, Damien Simone chose to stay. Even before the first signature, even before the estate existed, there was this plot, worked alone, by hand, out of conviction.

Today, in its honor, we give its name to our entire range.



Discover the story of Le Calvaire

domain.text:
2023, Domaine Simone officially acquires its first plot. The official beginning of a story that, in reality, had started long before.



No inherited vines, no handed-down cellar, no name already written on a label. Only a desire: that of a young man who wanted to make wine, and an ambition, that of making Geneva a benchmark.



Since then, this conviction has taken root and, with more than 1.4 hectares of vineyards in production, Domaine Simone does not perpetuate a story. It is writing one.

founders.alt: Photo of Damien Simone and Natalio Cano
founders.text:
Damien Simone spent months traveling across the canton in search of land that could carry his ambition. It was during this quest that he met, and then convinced, Natalio Cano. Two profiles, one shared ambition.



Today, Damien manages the vines and the wine, from the plot to the bottle. Natalio carries the voice of the estate to the market, in Switzerland as well as internationally. Two professions, a single goal: to put Geneva on the map.



From this encounter, Domaine Simone was born. Not an estate that perpetuates a family history, but an estate that creates one.

home.imageAlt: Coat of arms of Domaine Simone
home.title: Domaine Simone does not perpetuate a story. It is writing one.

wine.alt: Photo of the bottle of Sélection white wine
wine.text:
5473 bottles of this elegant and complex white wine, born from the harmonious alliance between Chasselas and Chardonnay.



On the nose, the floral aromas of the Chasselas (linden, white flowers) converse with the fruits of the Chardonnay (pear, white peach), enriched by a minerality characteristic of moraine soils and discreet tertiary aromas (butter, brioche, vanilla, roasted nuts).



On the palate, the balance between the mineral tension of the Chasselas and the roundness of the Chardonnay is confirmed. The absence of filtration preserves the authenticity of the terroir.



A beautiful, lingering, and crystalline finish.

men.alt: Photo of a young woman drinking a glass of wine with Damien Simone in the background

damien.text:
No family estate. No inheritance. Just a rigorous training at and years spent learning to know the Geneva terroir in order to sublimate it.



He tends the vines alone, crafts each cuvée with precision. A judge in the most demanding competitions in the wine world (Sélection des Vins de Genève, Concours Général Agricole de Paris, Grand Prix des Vins Suisses, International Wine Challenge in London), he knows excellence. The kind you find in his own wine.
damien.title: Damien Simone – Oenologist

men.intro: Two men. One shared conviction: that Geneva could produce a great wine. Not just a nice local wine. A wine that has nothing to envy anyone.

natalio.text: He structures, weaves connections, opens doors. He ensures that every bottle finds the table it deserves: restaurateurs, wine merchants, export markets. Rigorous, methodical, deeply invested in the estate's success.
natalio.title: Natalio Cano – Sales & Export

men.outro: Together, they chose Russin. Not by default, but by conviction. An appellation with some of the strictest rules in the canton, on slopes that give no quarter. Here, nothing is easy. That is exactly what they came looking for.
men.title: You don't build a reputation to sell wine. You make wine. The rest follows.

content.p1: Domaine Simone works with a restricted circle of selected establishments. No mass distribution, no compromise on placement.
content.p2: Our cuvées are produced in confidential, numbered quantities, which requires us to be rigorous in the choice of our partners.
content.p3: What we offer you is a de facto exclusivity: a wine that your customers will not find everywhere, carried by a story that your team can make their own in the dining room or in the shop.

intro.p1: Three grape varieties, a single plot, 36 months in barrels and 12 months in the bottle. This wine is the result of an uncompromising winemaking protocol, which can be felt in the glass.
intro.p2: Deep blackish-purple. A nose of candied black fruits, blackcurrant and blackberry, with sweet spices, violet, and a well-integrated smoky touch. On the palate, the attack is broad and silky, the tannic structure dense and noble, the finish long and saline. The 40% slope does the rest: an aromatic and phenolic ripeness that no technique can replace.
intro.p3: The cornerstone of the project, the Grande Réserve cuvée expresses the Estate's desire to create a wine that is at once a work of art, a strong brand, and an international benchmark.

properties.harvest: Hand-harvested in 13 kg crates, on September 16th, 24th and 25th
properties.maturing: 36 months in new white oak and French oak barrels and 12 months in bottle
properties.soil: Clay-silt with abundant colluvium, from Quaternary glacial deposits (moraines)
properties.variety: 33% Merlot, 33% Cabernet Sauvignon and 34% Cabernet Franc

reserve.quote: We make no compromises. We make wine.

intro.p1: 59% Chasselas, 41% Chardonnay. Six months on the lees, a fraction in barrels. The result is a dry, precise white, with tension and depth—something not necessarily expected from Geneva. That is exactly the point.
intro.p2: In the glass: a luminous golden color. The nose is floral and fruity (white flowers, peach, citrus) with a slight buttery touch brought by the aging. On the palate, the attack is fresh and lively, the mid-palate rich and textured, the finish long and mineral. A gastronomic wine, made to accompany food.

properties.alcoholicFermentation: In stainless steel tank at low temperature (< 20°C)
properties.consumption: Drink now, or cellar for 5 to 8 years
properties.harvest: Hand-harvested in 13 kg crates, on September 8th and 11th
properties.maturing: 6 months on fine lees with regular stirring, in stainless steel tank, with a fraction (16% of the Chardonnay) aged in new white oak barrels (Quercus Alba).
properties.soil: Clay-silt with abundant colluvium, from Quaternary glacial deposits (moraines)

selection.quote: This wine tells a story: that of an underestimated terroir and a man determined to reveal it.
